lines.english: Lesson 60
lines.chinese: 第60课
lines.raw: Lesson 60 | 第60课

lines.english: Too early and too late
lines.chinese: 为时过早和为时过晚
lines.raw: Too early and too late | 为时过早和为时过晚

lines.english: Why did the young girl miss the train?
lines.chinese: 小女孩为什么错过了火车？
lines.raw: Why did the young girl miss the train? | 小女孩为什么错过了火车？

lines.english: Punctuality is a necessary habit in all public affairs in civilized society.
lines.chinese: 在文明社会中，准时是处理一切公共事务的必要习惯。
lines.raw: Punctuality is a necessary habit in all public affairs in civilized society. | 在文明社会中，准时是处理一切公共事务的必要习惯。

lines.english: Without it, nothing could ever be brought to a conclusion; everything would be in a state of chaos.
lines.chinese: 没有它，任何事情都无法成功；一切都将处于混乱之中。
lines.raw: Without it, nothing could ever be brought to a conclusion; everything would be in a state of chaos. | 没有它，任何事情都无法成功；一切都将处于混乱之中。

lines.english: Only in a sparsely-populated rural community is it possible to disregard it.
lines.chinese: 只有在人口稀少的乡村社区，才有可能忽视它。
lines.raw: Only in a sparsely-populated rural community is it possible to disregard it. | 只有在人口稀少的乡村社区，才有可能忽视它。

lines.english: In ordinary living, there can be some tolerance of unpunctuality.
lines.chinese: 在日常生活中，对不准时可以有一些宽容。
lines.raw: In ordinary living, there can be some tolerance of unpunctuality. | 在日常生活中，对不准时可以有一些宽容。

lines.english: The intellectual, who is working on some abstruse problem, has everything coordinated and organized for the matter in hand.
lines.chinese: 正在研究某个深奥问题的知识分子，会把手头的一切事物都协调组织好。
lines.raw: The intellectual, who is working on some abstruse problem, has everything coordinated and organized for the matter in hand. | 正在研究某个深奥问题的知识分子，会把手头的一切事物都协调组织好。

lines.english: He is therefore forgiven if late for a dinner party.
lines.chinese: 因此，如果他赴晚宴迟到了，是可以被原谅的。
lines.raw: He is therefore forgiven if late for a dinner party. | 因此，如果他赴晚宴迟到了，是可以被原谅的。

lines.english: But people are often reproached for unpunctuality when their only fault is cutting things fine.
lines.chinese: 但人们常常因为不准时而受到指责，尽管他们唯一的过错只是把时间安排得太紧凑。
lines.raw: But people are often reproached for unpunctuality when their only fault is cutting things fine. | 但人们常常因为不准时而受到指责，尽管他们唯一的过错只是把时间安排得太紧凑。

lines.english: It is hard for energetic, quick-minded people to waste time,
lines.chinese: 对于精力充沛、头脑敏捷的人来说，浪费时间是很难的，
lines.raw: It is hard for energetic, quick-minded people to waste time, | 对于精力充沛、头脑敏捷的人来说，浪费时间是很难的，

lines.english: so they are often tempted to finish a job before setting out to keep an appointment.
lines.chinese: 所以他们常常想在赴约之前完成一项工作。
lines.raw: so they are often tempted to finish a job before setting out to keep an appointment. | 所以他们常常想在赴约之前完成一项工作。

lines.english: If no accidents occur on the way, like punctured tyres, diversions of traffic, sudden descent of fog, they will be on time.
lines.chinese: 如果路上没有发生意外，比如爆胎、交通改道、突如其来的大雾，他们就会准时到达。
lines.raw: If no accidents occur on the way, like punctured tyres, diversions of traffic, sudden descent of fog, they will be on time. | 如果路上没有发生意外，比如爆胎、交通改道、突如其来的大雾，他们就会准时到达。

lines.english: They are often more industrious, useful citizens than those who are never late.
lines.chinese: 他们往往比那些从不迟到的人更勤劳、更有用。
lines.raw: They are often more industrious, useful citizens than those who are never late. | 他们往往比那些从不迟到的人更勤劳、更有用。

lines.english: The over-punctual can be as much a trial to others as the unpunctual.
lines.chinese: 过分准时的人和不准时的人一样，都会给别人带来麻烦。
lines.raw: The over-punctual can be as much a trial to others as the unpunctual. | 过分准时的人和不准时的人一样，都会给别人带来麻烦。

lines.english: The guest who arrives half an hour too soon is the greatest nuisance.
lines.chinese: 提前半小时到达的客人是最让人讨厌的。
lines.raw: The guest who arrives half an hour too soon is the greatest nuisance. | 提前半小时到达的客人是最让人讨厌的。

lines.english: Some friends of my family had this irritating habit.
lines.chinese: 我家里有些朋友就有这种令人恼火的习惯。
lines.raw: Some friends of my family had this irritating habit. | 我家里有些朋友就有这种令人恼火的习惯。

lines.english: The only thing to do was ask them to come half an hour later than the other guests.
lines.chinese: 唯一的办法就是请他们比其他客人晚半小时来。
lines.raw: The only thing to do was ask them to come half an hour later than the other guests. | 唯一的办法就是请他们比其他客人晚半小时来。

lines.english: Then they arrived just when we wanted them.
lines.chinese: 这样他们就能在我们想要的时候到达。
lines.raw: Then they arrived just when we wanted them. | 这样他们就能在我们想要的时候到达。

lines.english: If you are catching a train, it is always better to be comfortably early than even a fraction of a minute too late.
lines.chinese: 如果你要赶火车，最好是提前充足时间，而不是哪怕晚一分钟。
lines.raw: If you are catching a train, it is always better to be comfortably early than even a fraction of a minute too late. | 如果你要赶火车，最好是提前充足时间，而不是哪怕晚一分钟。

lines.english: Although being early may mean wasting a little time,
lines.chinese: 尽管提前到达可能会浪费一点时间，
lines.raw: Although being early may mean wasting a little time, | 尽管提前到达可能会浪费一点时间，

lines.english: this will be less than if you miss the train and have to wait an hour or more for the next one;
lines.chinese: 但这比你错过火车，不得不等一个多小时才能搭乘下一班车要好；
lines.raw: this will be less than if you miss the train and have to wait an hour or more for the next one; | 但这比你错过火车，不得不等一个多小时才能搭乘下一班车要好；

lines.english: and you avoid the frustration of arriving at the very moment when the train is drawing out of the station and being unable to get on it.
lines.chinese: 而且你还能避免那种火车正驶出车站而你却无法上车的沮丧。
lines.raw: and you avoid the frustration of arriving at the very moment when the train is drawing out of the station and being unable to get on it. | 而且你还能避免那种火车正驶出车站而你却无法上车的沮丧。

lines.english: An even harder situation is to be on the platform in good time for a train and still to see it go off without you.
lines.chinese: 更令人恼火的情况是，你准时到了站台，却眼睁睁看着火车没有你开走。
lines.raw: An even harder situation is to be on the platform in good time for a train and still to see it go off without you. | 更令人恼火的情况是，你准时到了站台，却眼睁睁看着火车没有你开走。

lines.english: Such an experience befell a certain young girl the first time she was travelling alone.
lines.chinese: 这样的经历就发生在一个年轻女孩身上，那是她第一次独自旅行。
lines.raw: Such an experience befell a certain young girl the first time she was travelling alone. | 这样的经历就发生在一个年轻女孩身上，那是她第一次独自旅行。

lines.english: She entered the station twenty minutes before the train was due,
lines.chinese: 她在火车到站前20分钟就进了车站，
lines.raw: She entered the station twenty minutes before the train was due, | 她在火车到站前20分钟就进了车站，

lines.english: since her parents had impressed upon her
lines.chinese: 因为她的父母曾告诫她
lines.raw: since her parents had impressed upon her | 因为她的父母曾告诫她

lines.english: that it would be unforgivable to miss it and cause the friends with whom she was going to stay to make two journeys to meet her.
lines.chinese: 错过火车是不可原谅的，那会让她要去拜访的朋友来接她两次。
lines.raw: that it would be unforgivable to miss it and cause the friends with whom she was going to stay to make two journeys to meet her. | 错过火车是不可原谅的，那会让她要去拜访的朋友来接她两次。

lines.english: She gave her luggage to a porter and showed him her ticket.
lines.chinese: 她把行李交给一个搬运工，并给他看了她的车票。
lines.raw: She gave her luggage to a porter and showed him her ticket. | 她把行李交给一个搬运工，并给他看了她的车票。

lines.english: To her horror he said that she was two hours too soon.
lines.chinese: 让她吃惊的是，他说她早来了两个小时。
lines.raw: To her horror he said that she was two hours too soon. | 让她吃惊的是，他说她早来了两个小时。

lines.english: She felt in her handbag for the piece of paper on which her father had written down all the details of the journey and gave it to the porter.
lines.chinese: 她从手提包里摸索出父亲写下所有旅行细节的纸条，交给了搬运工。
lines.raw: She felt in her handbag for the piece of paper on which her father had written down all the details of the journey and gave it to the porter. | 她从手提包里摸索出父亲写下所有旅行细节的纸条，交给了搬运工。

lines.english: He agreed that a train did come into the station at the time on the paper and that it did stop, but only to take on mail, not passengers.
lines.chinese: 他承认火车确实在纸上写的时间到达车站，也确实停靠了，但那只是为了装载邮件，而不是载客。
lines.raw: He agreed that a train did come into the station at the time on the paper and that it did stop, but only to take on mail, not passengers. | 他承认火车确实在纸上写的时间到达车站，也确实停靠了，但那只是为了装载邮件，而不是载客。

lines.english: The girl asked to see a timetable, feeling sure that her father could not have made such a mistake.
lines.chinese: 女孩要求看时刻表，她确信她父亲不可能犯这样的错误。
lines.raw: The girl asked to see a timetable, feeling sure that her father could not have made such a mistake. | 女孩要求看时刻表，她确信她父亲不可能犯这样的错误。

lines.english: The porter went to fetch one and arrived back with the station master,
lines.chinese: 搬运工去拿时刻表，回来时带着站长，
lines.raw: The porter went to fetch one and arrived back with the station master, | 搬运工去拿时刻表，回来时带着站长，

lines.english: who produced it with a flourish and pointed out a microscopic 'o' beside the time of the arrival of the train at his station;
lines.chinese: 站长得意地拿出时刻表，指出了他们车站火车到站时间旁边的一个微小的“o”；
lines.raw: who produced it with a flourish and pointed out a microscopic 'o' beside the time of the arrival of the train at his station; | 站长得意地拿出时刻表，指出了他们车站火车到站时间旁边的一个微小的“o”；

lines.english: this little 'o' indicated that the train only stopped for mail.
lines.chinese: 这个小小的“o”表示火车只停靠装载邮件。
lines.raw: this little 'o' indicated that the train only stopped for mail. | 这个小小的“o”表示火车只停靠装载邮件。

lines.english: Just as that moment the train came into the station.
lines.chinese: 就在那时，火车进站了。
lines.raw: Just as that moment the train came into the station. | 就在那时，火车进站了。

lines.english: The girl, tears streaming down her face, begged to be allowed to slip into the guard's van.
lines.chinese: 女孩泪流满面，乞求允许她溜进守车。
lines.raw: The girl, tears streaming down her face, begged to be allowed to slip into the guard's van. | 女孩泪流满面，乞求允许她溜进守车。

lines.english: But the station master was adamant: rules could not be broken.
lines.chinese: 但站长态度坚决：规则不能打破。
lines.raw: But the station master was adamant: rules could not be broken. | 但站长态度坚决：规则不能打破。

lines.english: And she had to watch that train disappear towards her destination while she was left behind.
lines.chinese: 她不得不眼睁睁看着那列火车开往目的地，而她却被留在了原地。
lines.raw: And she had to watch that train disappear towards her destination while she was left behind. | 她不得不眼睁睁看着那列火车开往目的地，而她却被留在了原地。
